title: leptochloa dubia subsp. patens
intro: Leptochloa dubia subsp. patens, also known as green panic grass or panic grass, is a type of grass native to Australia and other parts of the world, characterized by its broad leaves and greenish-yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Leptochloa dubia subsp. patens include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or applying them topically to affected areas.
